title: External Integrations, Distributed Effects, and Resilience · Cumulative Product Review
summary: A delayed review reconstructs external integrations, distributed effects, and resilience from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for controlled integration and resilience evidence after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Document provider purpose, authority, data, credentials, terms, version, quota, availability, cost, accessibility, replacement, and exit boundaries.
Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.
Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Vendor Exit
steps.instruction:
review case — A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.

Inspect the Vendor Exit trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Vendor Exit.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Vendor Exit, keep Vendor Exit connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Vendor Exit, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Vendor Exit, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Vendor Exit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Vendor Exit, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Vendor Exit, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: An interface abstraction makes vendor exit inexpensive.

stimulus.title: Vendor Exit case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An interface abstraction makes vendor exit inexpensive.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider. Artifact under review: controlled integration and resilience evidence.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Vendor Exit, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Integration Authority
steps.instruction:
Order the Integration Authority evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Integration Authority.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Integration Authority assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Integration Authority, keep Integration Authority connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Vendor Exit
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Vendor Exit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Vendor Exit.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Vendor Exit, keep Vendor Exit connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Rehearse export, replacement, key rotation, data deletion, contract termination, user communication, and degraded operation without the provider.
